title: Puerto Banus Yacht Charter 2026: Prices and Tips
html:
Planning a yacht charter in Puerto Banus for 2026 means entering Europe's most glamorous marina on the Costa del Sol, where the Mediterranean Sea sparkles between Marbella and Gibraltar. Rental prices typically range from €250 for a half-day motorboat to €5,000 for a crewed superyacht, and BoatHire24 lists verified options across every budget.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus itself is the main departure point, with its own marina holding hundreds of yachts. Nearby Puerto José Banus offers additional berths, while Marbella's Club del Mar and the fishing port of La Bajadilla provide alternative launch spots in the town. For longer day trips, you can head to the upmarket marina of Sotogrande or anchor off the exclusive island of Isla del Soto. The Bay of Gibraltar is a popular destination, roughly 30 nautical miles south, where you can watch tankers and dolphins.

Most charters start from the main Puerto Banus marina, where BoatHire24 lists skippered charters in Puerto Banus that include fuel, insurance and a professional captain. The marina's location lets you explore the Costa del Sol coast quickly, with calm morning seas ideal for families.

Which boat to rent

  1. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open: a 6-metre runabout ideal for coastal cruising, seating up to 6 guests, from €350 for a half-day. See the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP charter page for availability.
  2. Azimut 45 Flybridge: a 14-metre motor yacht with flybridge, perfect for a full-day family trip, accommodating up to 10 guests, from €1,200.
  3. Lagoon 450 Catamaran: a 13-metre sailing catamaran with four cabins, great for a week-long liveaboard charter, sleeps 8, from €3,500 per week.
  4. Sunseeker Manhattan 52: a 16-metre luxury motor yacht with crew, suitable for corporate events, comfortable for 12 guests, from €2,800 for a day.
  5. Axopar 37 T-Top: a 11-metre semi-rigid offshore cruiser, ideal for water sports, holds 8 guests, from €900 for a full day.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Puerto Banus?

Prices range from €250 for a shared tour to over €5,000 for a fully crewed superyacht per day. The final cost depends on boat size, season, duration and extras like water toys or catering.

  • Shared group tour on a motor yacht: from €85 per person for 3 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day (6, 8 guests): €250, €500
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): €1,500, €2,500
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (10, 15 guests): €3,000, €5,000

Do you need a licence to charter a boat in Puerto Banus?

In Spain, you need a recreational boating licence for any boat over 5 metres or with more than 15 hp, unless you hire a skipper. Most holidaymakers choose skippered charters to avoid paperwork. All operators on BoatHire24 confirm the exact requirements for each boat, so you know before you book.

If you plan to sail from Puerto Banus to Gibraltar, book a skipper who knows the strong easterly Levanter wind, which can pick up suddenly in the afternoon. Morning trips are typically calmer and more relaxing.

At a glance

  • Price band: €250, €5,000 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 3 hours to 7 days
  • Guest capacity: 6, 15 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a yacht without a licence in Puerto Banus?

Yes, you can hire a skippered yacht without any licence, and this is the most common choice for visitors.

What is the best month for a yacht charter in Puerto Banus?

June and September are ideal because the weather is warm, the water is calm, and the marina is less crowded than July and August.

How far is it from Puerto Banus to Gibraltar by sea?

The distance is approximately 30 nautical miles, taking about 1.5 to 2 hours in a fast motor yacht.

Can I rent a boat for a week in Puerto Banus?

Yes, many catamarans and motor yachts are available for weekly charters, with prices starting around €3,500 for a Lagoon 450.

Whether you want a 3-hour coastal cruise or a week-long exploration of the Costa del Sol, the BoatHire24 marketplace connects you directly with local owners and charter companies. If you own a boat in Puerto Banus, you can also list your own vessel and earn income from the growing demand for self-drive and skippered charters in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
